modules.text: FinRecon is a financial data reconciliation engine built at Bank of America for EMBS (Enterprise Mortgage-Backed Securities). It automates reconciliation across 4 distributed database systems (Sybase IQ, Sybase ASE, Oracle, Netezza) with anomaly detection and comprehensive runbook documentation.

modules.title: The Challenge
modules.text: Bank of America processes millions of mortgage-backed securities records monthly across Freddie Mac, Fannie Mae, and Ginnie Mae agency pools. Manual reconciliation was time-consuming, error-prone, and created bottlenecks in critical production cycles. The system needed to handle 100,000+ record datasets with sub-second query performance.

modules.title: Solution & Impact
modules.text: Built an automated reconciliation engine with intelligent anomaly detection across 4 database systems. Implemented sophisticated SQL query optimization through indexing and batch chunking strategies. Reduced manual overhead by ~60% and improved query performance by ~45%.

modules.title: Key Features
modules.text: Multi-source data reconciliation with anomaly detection, comprehensive runbook-style documentation for incident response, batch processing for 100,000+ record datasets, intelligent indexing strategies, and detailed audit trails. The system achieved zero SLA breaches across an 18-month tenure.
